---
title: "Ayon Ganguly"
image: "me-croped.JPG"
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:aganguly@iitg.ac.in
    # - icon: github
    #   text: GitHub
    #   href: https://github.com/ayonganguly
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=MN2c440AAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: /assets/CV_Ayon_Ganguly.pdf
---

# Bio

I am an assistant professor at the [Department of Mathematics](https://www.iitg.ac.in/maths/){target="_blank"}, [Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati](https://www.iitg.ac.in/){target="_blank"}. Before moving
to Guwahati, I worked as assistant professor at [Indian Institute of Information
Technology, Allahabad](https://www.iiita.ac.in/){target="_blank"}. I did my PhD
in Statistics from the [Department of Mathematics and
Statistics](https://www.iitk.ac.in/math/){target="_blank"}, [Indian Institute of
Technology Kanpur](https://www.iitk.ac.in/){target="_blank"}, under the
supervision of [Prof. Debasis
Kundu](http://home.iitk.ac.in/~kundu/kundu.html){target="_blank"} and [Prof.
Sharmishtha Mitra](http://home.iitk.ac.in/~smitra/){target="_blank"}.

---

# Research Interests

* Survival Analysis & Reliability Theory
* Finite Mixture Distributions
* Frailty Models
* Competing Risks
* Accelerated Life Tests

<!-- --- -->
<!--  -->
<!--  -->
<!-- ## Selected Publications -->
<!--  -->
<!-- 1. **Ganguly, A.**, et al. *"Analysis of Censored and Competing Risks Data in Reliability."* *Journal Name*, 202X. [[DOI]](#) [[PDF]](#) [[Code]](#) -->
<!-- 2. **Ganguly, A.**, et al. *"Frailty Modeling for Accelerated Life Tests."* *Journal Name*, 202X. [[DOI]](#) [[PDF]](#) -->
<!--  -->
<!-- --- -->
<!--  -->
<!--  -->
<!-- ## News & Updates -->
<!--  -->
<!-- * **July 2026:** Presenting at the *Recent Advances in Applied Mathematics (RAAM)* conference. -->
<!-- * **Spring 2026:** Currently teaching **MA324** (Statistical Inference) and **MA212M** (Mathematical Statistics). -->


<!-- ::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"} -->
<!-- ## Note to Prospective Students -->
<!-- I am always looking for motivated PhD students and researchers with a strong background in mathematical statistics, probability theory, or computational statistics in R. If you are interested in working with me, please send me an email with your CV. -->
<!-- ::: -->
